content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

content.text:
Excellent! I have a comprehensive understanding now. Let me create a detailed CLAUDE.md report:

## Summary

I've thoroughly explored the dicom-stl codebase and can provide you with a comprehensive guide for development.

## THOROUGH UNDERSTANDING OF THE PROJECT

### 1. **Project Overview**

This is **3D Slicer (MVP Edition)** - a specialized build of the open-source 3D Slicer medical imaging platform, reduced to a minimal viable product (MVP) focused on a specific DICOM to STL segmentation workflow.

**Key points:**
- **Version:** 5.11.0 (Slicer)
- **Language:** C++ (core), Python (scripting, modules), CMake (build)
- **License:** Apache 2.0 (see LICENSE.txt and COPYRIGHT.txt)
- **Platform:** Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux) - currently configured for macOS
- **Repository:** GitHub (custom fork of Slicer/Slicer)

**Purpose:** Convert DICOM medical imaging data to segmentations and export to STL format for 3D visualization and analysis.

---

### 2. **How to Build**

**Prerequisites (macOS):**
```bash
brew install cmake qt@5
xcode-select --install  # Xcode Command Line Tools
```

**One-Command Build + DMG:**
```bash
mkdir -p build-mvp && \
cmake -S . -B build-mvp \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON \
  -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON \
  -DQt5_DIR=$(brew --prefix qt@5)/lib/cmake/Qt5 && \
cmake --build build-mvp --parallel $(sysctl -n hw.ncpu) && \
cmake --build build-mvp/Slicer-build --target package --parallel $(sysctl -n hw.ncpu)
```

**Output DMG:** `build-mvp/Slicer-build/Slicer-5.11.0-YYYY-MM-DD-macosx-arm64.dmg`

**Incremental Rebuild (after source changes):**
```bash
cmake --build build-mvp/Slicer-build --parallel $(sysctl -n hw.ncpu)
```

**Key CMake Options:**
- `-DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON` - Enable MVP-specific build configuration
- `-DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON` - Build external dependencies first
- `-DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release` - Optimized build
- `-DQt5_DIR=...` - Qt5 location (required)

---

### 3. **How to Run Tests**

**Testing Infrastructure:**
- **Test Framework:** CTest (CMake testing)
- **Python Tests:** Located in `/Testing/` directory
- **Test Configuration:** Controlled by `BUILD_TESTING` CMake option (default ON)

**Simple Test:**
```bash
ctest -C Release  # Run all tests
ctest -C Release -R "specific_test_name"  # Run specific test
```

**Python Test Example:**
- `Testing/SimpleNUMPYTest.py` - Tests NumPy integration

**CI/CD Tests:**
- GitHub Actions runs tests on every push to `main` and pull requests
- Tests configured in `.github/workflows/lint.yml` (code quality)
- Commit message validation in `.github/workflows/commit-message.yml`
- Builds validated in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (macOS DMG build)

---

### 4. **Linting & Code Formatting**

**Tools Used:**
1. **Python Linting:** `ruff` (configured in `.ruff.toml`, Python 3.12+ target)
2. **C++ Formatting:** `clang-format` (configured in `.clang-format`, C++17 standard)
3. **Pre-commit Hooks:** Multiple hooks defined in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`

**Key Linting Rules (Ruff):**
- Line length: 320 characters (very permissive)
- Enabled checks: B, C4, COM, E/F/W, D, ICN, ISC, NPY, PGH, PL, Q, RET, RUF, UP, YTT
- Extensive exclusions for legacy/test code

**C++ Formatting:**
- Based on Mozilla/LLVM style
- Column limit: 180
- C++17 standard
- Allman braces

**Pre-commit Hooks:**
```yaml
- check-added-large-files (max 1024KB)
- check-yaml, check-merge-conflict
- clang-format (C/C++ files: .cpp, .cxx, .h, .hpp, .hxx, .txx)
- ruff-check (Python, with --fix)
- pyupgrade (Python 3.12+)
- prettier (YAML formatting)
- check-github-workflows and check-dependabot
```

**Run Linting:**
```bash
pre-commit run --all-files  # Run all checks
ruff check --fix .         # Auto-fix Python issues
clang-format -i *.cpp      # Format C++ files
```

**CI Linting Workflow:** `.github/workflows/lint.yml` runs pre-commit on every push/PR

---

### 5. **High-Level Architecture**

**Major Components:**

```
Slicer (MVP Edition)
├── Libs/                    # Core libraries
│   ├── MRML/               # Medical Reality Markup Language (data model)
│   │   ├── Core/           # MRML core (vtkMRMLScene, nodes)
│   │   ├── Logic/          # Business logic layer
│   │   ├── Widgets/        # Qt widgets for MRML
│   │   ├── CLI/            # Command-line interface
│   │   └── DisplayableManager/
│   ├── vtkSegmentationCore/# Segmentation algorithms
│   ├── vtkITK/             # ITK (Insight Toolkit) integration
│   ├── vtkTeem/            # Teem (scientific visualization)
│   ├── RemoteIO/           # Remote file I/O
│   ├── ITKFactoryRegistration/  # ITK plugin registration
│   └── krb5-gssapi-stub/   # GSSAPI stub for Qt compatibility
│
├── Base/                    # Application base layer
│   ├── Logic/              # Core application logic
│   ├── CLI/                # CLI launcher
│   ├── QTCLI/              # Qt CLI widgets
│   ├── QTCore/             # Qt core components
│   ├── QTGUI/              # Main application GUI (129 subdirectories!)
│   ├── QTApp/              # Qt application wrapper
│   └── Python/             # Python API bindings
│
├── Modules/                 # Feature modules
│   ├── Core/               # Core event broker
│   ├── Loadable/           # Qt Loadable modules (load at runtime)
│   │   ├── Volumes
│   │   ├── VolumeRendering
│   │   ├── Segmentations
│   │   ├── Markups
│   │   ├── Transforms
│   │   ├── Annotations
│   │   ├── SubjectHierarchy
│   │   ├── Terminologies
│   │   ├── Colors
│   │   ├── Units
│   │   ├── SceneViews
│   │   ├── Sequences
│   │   ├── GeneralizedReformat
│   │   └── Cameras
│   ├── Scripted/           # Python-based modules
│   │   ├── DICOM           # DICOM import/management (MVP-critical)
│   │   ├── DICOMLib        # DICOM library utilities
│   │   ├── DICOMPlugins    # DICOM importer plugins
│   │   └── SegmentEditor   # Segmentation editing UI (MVP-critical)
│   └── CLI/                # Command-line modules
│       └── CreateDICOMSeries  # DICOM generation (MVP required)
│
├── Applications/           # Standalone applications
├── Resources/              # Icons, assets, resources
├── Docs/                   # Documentation (Sphinx/Read the Docs)
├── CMake/                  # Build system (114 files!)
├── SuperBuild/             # External dependency management
└── Utilities/              # Scripts and tools
```

**Component Interactions:**
```
DICOM Files
    ↓
DICOMLib + DICOMPlugins (Python scripting)
    ↓
MRML/Core (data model) ← vtkSegmentationCore
    ↓
SegmentEditor (UI module)
    ↓
Segmentations + VolumeRendering (Loadable modules)
    ↓
STL Export (via vtkSegmentationCore)
    ↓
STL Files
```

**Critical Technologies:**
- **VTK:** Visualization Toolkit (3D rendering)
- **ITK:** Insight Toolkit (image processing)
- **Qt5/Qt6:** GUI framework
- **MRML:** Proprietary data model (replaces traditional file formats)
- **Python:** Scripting and high-level module logic
- **DCMTK:** DICOM Toolkit (handling DICOM format)

---

### 6. **Key Directories & Their Purposes**

| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/Libs/MRML/` | Core data model - scene, nodes, storage |
| `/Libs/vtkSegmentationCore/` | Segmentation algorithms and data structures |
| `/Libs/vtkITK/`, `/Libs/vtkTeem/` | Third-party toolkit integration |
| `/Base/QTGUI/` | Main application GUI (very large, 129 subdirs) |
| `/Base/QTCore/` | Qt core infrastructure |
| `/Modules/Loadable/` | Dynamically-loaded feature modules |
| `/Modules/Scripted/DICOM*` | DICOM workflow (MVP-essential) |
| `/Modules/Scripted/SegmentEditor/` | Segmentation UI (MVP-essential) |
| `/CMake/` | Build system helpers and macros (114 files) |
| `/SuperBuild/` | External dependency configuration (VTK, ITK, Qt, Python, etc.) |
| `/Docs/` | Sphinx documentation (RTD-ready) |
| `/Testing/` | Python tests |
| `/Utilities/Scripts/` | Development scripts (setup, hooks, etc.) |

---

### 7. **CI/CD Configuration**

**GitHub Actions Workflows** (`.github/workflows/`):

| Workflow | Trigger | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `ci.yml` | Push to `main`, manual | Build macOS DMG (arm64), 180-min timeout, uploads artifact |
| `lint.yml` | Push/PR to any branch | Run pre-commit checks (Python, C++, YAML, etc.) |
| `commit-message.yml` | Push/PR | Validate commit message format (BUG:, ENH:, etc.) |
| `codespell.yml` | Manual | Check for spelling errors |
| `build-windows.yml` | Manual | Build Windows binary (separate workflow) |
| `scorecard.yml` | Manual | OpenSSF security scorecard |

**Key Workflow Features:**
- Caching of SuperBuild dependencies (significant for faster rebuilds)
- Python 3.12 for linting
- Uses Ninja build generator on macOS
- Artifact retention: 7 days
- Pre-commit action integrated

**Commit Message Format** (required):
```
[PREFIX]: Brief description

Longer explanation if needed.

Examples:
- BUG: Fix crash when loading DICOM
- ENH: Add STL export option
- COMP: Fix Windows build error
- STYLE: Format code
- DOC: Update README
```

---

### 8. **Project-Specific Notes**

**MVP Build Configuration:**
The project includes a special MVP (Minimal Viable Product) build mode:

**Enabled for MVP:**
- DICOM support
- Segment Editor
- Segmentations, Volumes, VolumeRendering
- Transforms, Markups, Annotations, Colors
- SubjectHierarchy, Terminologies, Units
- SceneViews, Sequences, GeneralizedReformat, Cameras

**Disabled for MVP:**
- Extension Manager
- Application Update
- Diffusion/DTI support
- WebEngine
- SimpleITK, SciPy (only NumPy)
- Most unrelated CLI modules

**Key Configure Option:**
```cmake
-DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON
```

This triggers special UI hiding (in `/Base/QTGUI/`), module filtering, and default module selection.

---

### 9. **Development Workflow**

**Setup for Development:**
```bash
./Utilities/SetupForDevelopment.sh
```

This script:
1. Configures Git for development (rebase on pull)
2. Sets up Git hooks
3. Validates Git user configuration

**Python Version:** 3.12.10 (required)
**Qt Version:** 5.15 or 6.8
**CMake:** 3.20.6 to 3.24.x (avoid 3.21.0 and 3.25.0-3.25.2)
**C++ Standard:** C++17 (required, can use C++20)

**Code Style Guidelines** (from CONTRIBUTING.md):
- Follow Slicer commit message conventions
- Write tests for new features
- Consider backward compatibility
- Update documentation

---

### 10. **No Existing CLAUDE.md Found**

There is no existing `CLAUDE.md` or `.cursor*` file in the repository. This is your opportunity to establish development guidelines!

---

## RECOMMENDATIONS FOR CLAUDE.md

When you create a CLAUDE.md for this project, include:

1. **Quick Start:** MVP build command (1-2 min read)
2. **Code Organization:** Where DICOM, Segmentation, STL code lives
3. **Testing:** How to run MVP-specific tests
4. **Common Tasks:** Adding a new module, fixing DICOM bugs, tweaking UI
5. **MVP-Specific Rules:** Don't add dependencies, keep UI minimal
6. **Pre-commit Hooks:** How to use ruff/clang-format
7. **Debugging:** Using Python debugger in Slicer, GDB for C++
8. **Documentation:** How to update Docs/ and build locally
9. **Release Process:** How MVP builds are versioned/tagged
10. **Key Contacts:** Where to find DICOM, Segmentation, UI owners in code

```bash

ruff check --fix .         # Auto-fix Python issues
clang-format -i *.cpp      # Format C++ files
```

**CI Linting Workflow:** `.github/workflows/lint.yml` runs pre-commit on every push/PR

---

### 5. **High-Level Architecture**

**Major Components:**

```
Slicer (MVP Edition)
├── Libs/                    # Core libraries
│   ├── MRML/               # Medical Reality Markup Language (data model)
│   │   ├── Core/           # MRML core (vtkMRMLScene, nodes)
│   │   ├── Logic/          # Business logic layer
│   │   ├── Widgets/        # Qt widgets for MRML
│   │   ├── CLI/            # Command-line interface
│   │   └── DisplayableManager/
│   ├── vtkSegmentationCore/# Segmentation algorithms
│   ├── vtkITK/             # ITK (Insight Toolkit) integration
│   ├── vtkTeem/            # Teem (scientific visualization)
│   ├── RemoteIO/           # Remote file I/O
│   ├── ITKFactoryRegistration/  # ITK plugin registration
│   └── krb5-gssapi-stub/   # GSSAPI stub for Qt compatibility
│
├── Base/                    # Application base layer
│   ├── Logic/              # Core application logic
│   ├── CLI/                # CLI launcher
│   ├── QTCLI/              # Qt CLI widgets
│   ├── QTCore/             # Qt core components
│   ├── QTGUI/              # Main application GUI (129 subdirectories!)
│   ├── QTApp/              # Qt application wrapper
│   └── Python/             # Python API bindings
│
├── Modules/                 # Feature modules
│   ├── Core/               # Core event broker
│   ├── Loadable/           # Qt Loadable modules (load at runtime)
│   │   ├── Volumes
│   │   ├── VolumeRendering
│   │   ├── Segmentations
│   │   ├── Markups
│   │   ├── Transforms
│   │   ├── Annotations
│   │   ├── SubjectHierarchy
│   │   ├── Terminologies
│   │   ├── Colors
│   │   ├── Units
│   │   ├── SceneViews
│   │   ├── Sequences
│   │   ├── GeneralizedReformat
│   │   └── Cameras
│   ├── Scripted/           # Python-based modules
│   │   ├── DICOM           # DICOM import/management (MVP-critical)
│   │   ├── DICOMLib        # DICOM library utilities
│   │   ├── DICOMPlugins    # DICOM importer plugins
│   │   └── SegmentEditor   # Segmentation editing UI (MVP-critical)
│   └── CLI/                # Command-line modules
│       └── CreateDICOMSeries  # DICOM generation (MVP required)
│
├── Applications/           # Standalone applications
├── Resources/              # Icons, assets, resources
├── Docs/                   # Documentation (Sphinx/Read the Docs)
├── CMake/                  # Build system (114 files!)
├── SuperBuild/             # External dependency management
└── Utilities/              # Scripts and tools
```

**Component Interactions:**
```
DICOM Files
    ↓
DICOMLib + DICOMPlugins (Python scripting)
    ↓
MRML/Core (data model) ← vtkSegmentationCore
    ↓
SegmentEditor (UI module)
    ↓
Segmentations + VolumeRendering (Loadable modules)
    ↓
STL Export (via vtkSegmentationCore)
    ↓
STL Files
```

**Critical Technologies:**
- **VTK:** Visualization Toolkit (3D rendering)
- **ITK:** Insight Toolkit (image processing)
- **Qt5/Qt6:** GUI framework
- **MRML:** Proprietary data model (replaces traditional file formats)
- **Python:** Scripting and high-level module logic
- **DCMTK:** DICOM Toolkit (handling DICOM format)

---

### 6. **Key Directories & Their Purposes**

| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/Libs/MRML/` | Core data model - scene, nodes, storage |
| `/Libs/vtkSegmentationCore/` | Segmentation algorithms and data structures |
| `/Libs/vtkITK/`, `/Libs/vtkTeem/` | Third-party toolkit integration |
| `/Base/QTGUI/` | Main application GUI (very large, 129 subdirs) |
| `/Base/QTCore/` | Qt core infrastructure |
| `/Modules/Loadable/` | Dynamically-loaded feature modules |
| `/Modules/Scripted/DICOM*` | DICOM workflow (MVP-essential) |
| `/Modules/Scripted/SegmentEditor/` | Segmentation UI (MVP-essential) |
| `/CMake/` | Build system helpers and macros (114 files) |
| `/SuperBuild/` | External dependency configuration (VTK, ITK, Qt, Python, etc.) |
| `/Docs/` | Sphinx documentation (RTD-ready) |
| `/Testing/` | Python tests |
| `/Utilities/Scripts/` | Development scripts (setup, hooks, etc.) |

---

### 7. **CI/CD Configuration**

**GitHub Actions Workflows** (`.github/workflows/`):

| Workflow | Trigger | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `ci.yml` | Push to `main`, manual | Build macOS DMG (arm64), 180-min timeout, uploads artifact |
| `lint.yml` | Push/PR to any branch | Run pre-commit checks (Python, C++, YAML, etc.) |
| `commit-message.yml` | Push/PR | Validate commit message format (BUG:, ENH:, etc.) |
| `codespell.yml` | Manual | Check for spelling errors |
| `build-windows.yml` | Manual | Build Windows binary (separate workflow) |
| `scorecard.yml` | Manual | OpenSSF security scorecard |

**Key Workflow Features:**
- Caching of SuperBuild dependencies (significant for faster rebuilds)
- Python 3.12 for linting
- Uses Ninja build generator on macOS
- Artifact retention: 7 days
- Pre-commit action integrated

**Commit Message Format** (required):
```
[PREFIX]: Brief description

Longer explanation if needed.

Examples:
- BUG: Fix crash when loading DICOM
- ENH: Add STL export option
- COMP: Fix Windows build error
- STYLE: Format code
- DOC: Update README
```

---

### 8. **Project-Specific Notes**

**MVP Build Configuration:**
The project includes a special MVP (Minimal Viable Product) build mode:

**Enabled for MVP:**
- DICOM support
- Segment Editor
- Segmentations, Volumes, VolumeRendering
- Transforms, Markups, Annotations, Colors
- SubjectHierarchy, Terminologies, Units
- SceneViews, Sequences, GeneralizedReformat, Cameras

**Disabled for MVP:**
- Extension Manager
- Application Update
- Diffusion/DTI support
- WebEngine
- SimpleITK, SciPy (only NumPy)
- Most unrelated CLI modules

**Key Configure Option:**
```cmake
-DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON
```

This triggers special UI hiding (in `/Base/QTGUI/`), module filtering, and default module selection.

---

### 9. **Development Workflow**

**Setup for Development:**
```bash
./Utilities/SetupForDevelopment.sh
```

This script:
1. Configures Git for development (rebase on pull)
2. Sets up Git hooks
3. Validates Git user configuration

**Python Version:** 3.12.10 (required)
**Qt Version:** 5.15 or 6.8
**CMake:** 3.20.6 to 3.24.x (avoid 3.21.0 and 3.25.0-3.25.2)
**C++ Standard:** C++17 (required, can use C++20)

**Code Style Guidelines** (from CONTRIBUTING.md):
- Follow Slicer commit message conventions
- Write tests for new features
- Consider backward compatibility
- Update documentation

---

### 10. **No Existing CLAUDE.md Found**

There is no existing `CLAUDE.md` or `.cursor*` file in the repository. This is your opportunity to establish development guidelines!

---

## RECOMMENDATIONS FOR CLAUDE.md

When you create a CLAUDE.md for this project, include:

1. **Quick Start:** MVP build command (1-2 min read)
2. **Code Organization:** Where DICOM, Segmentation, STL code lives
3. **Testing:** How to run MVP-specific tests
4. **Common Tasks:** Adding a new module, fixing DICOM bugs, tweaking UI
5. **MVP-Specific Rules:** Don't add dependencies, keep UI minimal
6. **Pre-commit Hooks:** How to use ruff/clang-format
7. **Debugging:** Using Python debugger in Slicer, GDB for C++
8. **Documentation:** How to update Docs/ and build locally
9. **Release Process:** How MVP builds are versioned/tagged
10. **Key Contacts:** Where to find DICOM, Segmentation, UI owners in code
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me create the CLAUDE.md file.

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What This Is

A specialized MVP build of 3D Slicer focused on a single workflow: loading DICOM medical imaging data, segmenting it, and exporting to STL. Most upstream Slicer features are disabled via `-DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON`.

## Build

**Full superbuild (first time):**
```bash
mkdir -p build-mvp
cmake -S . -B build-mvp \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON \
  -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON \
  -DQt5_DIR=$(brew --prefix qt@5)/lib/cmake/Qt5
cmake --build build-mvp --parallel $(sysctl -n hw.ncpu)
```

**Incremental rebuild after source changes:**
```bash
cmake --build build-mvp/Slicer-build --parallel $(sysctl -n hw.ncpu)
```

**Package (DMG):**
```bash
cmake --build build-mvp/Slicer-build --target package --parallel $(sysctl -n hw.ncpu)
```

**Requirements:** CMake 3.20.6–3.24.x (avoid 3.21.0 and 3.25.0–3.25.2), Qt 5.15, Python 3.12, Xcode CLT.

## Testing

```bash
ctest -C Release                          # all tests
ctest -C Release -R "specific_test_name" # single test
```

Python tests live in `Testing/`. C++ tests are registered via CTest in each module's CMakeLists.

## Linting & Formatting

```bash
pre-commit run --all-files    # run all checks
ruff check --fix .            # Python auto-fix
clang-format -i <file>.cpp   # format a C++ file
```

Config files: `.ruff.toml` (Python, line length 320), `.clang-format` (C++17, Mozilla/LLVM style, col limit 180), `.pre-commit-config.yaml`.

## Commit Message Format

CI enforces a prefix on every commit:
```
BUG: Fix crash when loading DICOM
ENH: Add STL export option
COMP: Fix Windows build error
STYLE: Format code
DOC: Update README
```

## Architecture

The core data flow for the MVP workflow:

```
DICOM Files → DICOMLib/DICOMPlugins (Python) → MRML scene (vtkMRMLScene)
    → SegmentEditor module (UI) → vtkSegmentationCore (algorithms)
    → STL Export
```

**Key layers:**

| Layer | Location | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Data model | `Libs/MRML/Core/` | Scene graph (vtkMRMLScene, MRML nodes) |
| Segmentation | `Libs/vtkSegmentationCore/` | Segmentation algorithms and data structures |
| App GUI | `Base/QTGUI/` | Main window, module panel, toolbars |
| DICOM workflow | `Modules/Scripted/DICOM*/` | DICOM import, database, plugins |
| Segmentation UI | `Modules/Scripted/SegmentEditor/` | MVP-critical segmentation editor |
| Loadable modules | `Modules/Loadable/` | Volumes, VolumeRendering, Segmentations, etc. |
| External deps | `SuperBuild/` | VTK, ITK, Qt, Python, DCMTK build configs |
| Build macros | `CMake/` | 100+ helper macros for Slicer's build system |

**MVP-disabled features** (do not re-enable unless intentional): Extension Manager, Application Update, WebEngine, SimpleITK, SciPy, most CLI modules, Diffusion/DTI support.

## MVP-Specific Customization

The flag `-DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON` controls:
- UI hiding logic in `Base/QTGUI/`
- Module filtering (which loadable/scripted modules compile)
- Default module selection on startup

When adding a new module, check `CMakeLists.txt` in `Modules/Loadable/` or `Modules/Scripted/` for the `Slicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP` guard pattern used by existing modules.

## Development Setup

```bash
./Utilities/SetupForDevelopment.sh   # configures git hooks, rebase-on-pull
```

message.content:
Actually the Windows build workflow This branch This branch  Not working. You have to fix the Windows build workflow. The job failed during the Slicer (SuperBuild) compile step on Windows because Ninja/Windows couldn’t spawn cl.exe for one of the build commands:

Log excerpt (failure point):
CreateProcess failed. Command attempted: ... cl.exe ...
ninja: fatal: CreateProcess: The parameter is incorrect.
(is the command line too long?)
This is a common Windows limitation: the generated compiler command line (lots of -D...The job is failing during the **Slicer build (Ninja on Windows defines/includes))** with:

CreateProcess failed... ninja: fatal: CreateProcess: The parameter is incorrect. (is the command line too long?) likely exceeded what CreateProcess` (or the Windows command-line length limit) will accept, so Ninja stops and the whole job exits with code 1.
You can see it in the job log around the first hard failure: https://github.com/yashs`

This is a classic Windows failure when the command line passed to33244/dicom-stl/actions/runs/25314306281/job/74208228861?pr=6 cl.exe exceeds Windows limits. Your workflow already tries to mitigate this via `-DCMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL=ON

Notes:

The earlier pthread / missing Linux headers messages are expected noise on Windows from cross-platform feature checks; they are not what caused the job to fail.
The actual terminal error is the , but the error still occurs insideCreateProcess/ command line too long** issue. Slicer’s build where **AUTOMOC/UIC and/or compile definitions/includes create extremely longcl.exe` invocations.

content.content:
1	name: Build Windows EXE
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches:
6	      - main
7	      - design
8	  workflow_dispatch:
9	
10	permissions:
11	  contents: read
12	
13	jobs:
14	  build-windows:
15	    name: Build Windows EXE
16	    runs-on: windows-2022
17	    timeout-minutes: 360
18	
19	    steps:
20	      - name: Free Disk Space (Windows)
21	        run: |
22	          Get-PSDrive
23	          # Remove large unnecessary components
24	          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
25	          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files (x86)\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
26	          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\CodeQL" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
27	          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Ruby" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
28	          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Go" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
29	          Get-PSDrive
30	
31	      - name: Checkout
32	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
33	        with:
34	          path: S
35	
36	      # MSVC + Ninja can exceed Win32 CreateProcess limits when paths repeat
37	      # (GitHub Actions default workspace is deep). SUBST yields short W:\ paths
38	      # embedded in VTK/Slicer ninja rules alongside CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE.
39	      - name: Map short workspace drive
40	        shell: pwsh
41	        run: subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE
42	
43	      - name: Set up MSVC
44	        uses: ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd@v1
45	
46	      - name: Install Qt
47	        uses: jurplel/install-qt-action@v3
48	        with:
49	          version: "5.15.2"
50	          arch: "win64_msvc2019_64"
51	          modules: "qtcharts qtwebengine"
52	
53	      - name: Debug Environment
54	        run: |
55	          dir env:
56	          echo "Qt5_Dir is: $env:Qt5_Dir"
57	
58	      - name: Install Ninja
59	        run: choco install ninja
60	
61	      - name: Configure SuperBuild
62	        run: |
63	          cmake -S W:\S -B W:\B `
64	            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release `
65	            -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON `
66	            -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON `
67	            -DSlicer_USE_SYSTEM_QT:BOOL=ON `
68	            -DCMAKE_GENERATOR=Ninja `
69	            -DCMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL=ON `
70	            -DSlicer_SKIP_ROOT_DIR_MAX_LENGTH_CHECK:BOOL=ON `
71	            -DSlicer_BUILD_BRAINSTOOLS:BOOL=OFF `
72	            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeExplorer:BOOL=OFF `
73	            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeImporter:BOOL=OFF `
74	            -DSlicer_BUILD_CompareVolumes:BOOL=OFF `
75	            -DSlicer_BUILD_LandmarkRegistration:BOOL=OFF `
76	            -DSlicer_BUILD_SurfaceToolbox:BOOL=OFF `
77	            -DSlicer_BUILD_SimpleFilters:BOOL=OFF `
78	            -DSlicer_USE_SimpleITK:BOOL=OFF `
79	            -DSlicer_USE_SCIPY:BOOL=OFF `
80	            -DQt5_DIR="$env:Qt5_Dir"
81	
82	      - name: Build SuperBuild
83	        run: cmake --build W:\B --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS
84	
85	      - name: Build package (EXE)
86	        run: |
87	          cmake --build W:\B\Slicer-build --target package --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS
88	
89	      - name: Find Installer
90	        id: find-installer
91	        shell: pwsh
92	        run: |
93	          $installer = Get-ChildItem -Path W:\B\Slicer-build -Filter "Slicer-*.exe" | Select-Object -First 1
94	          if ($installer) {
95	            echo "path=$($installer.FullName)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
96	            echo "name=$($installer.Name)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
97	          } else {
98	            Write-Error "Installer not found"
99	            exit 1
100	          }
101	
102	      - name: Upload EXE artifact
103	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
104	        with:
105	          name: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.name }}
106	          path: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.path }}
107	          retention-days: 7
108

file.content:
name: Build Windows EXE

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - design
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  build-windows:
    name: Build Windows EXE
    runs-on: windows-2022
    timeout-minutes: 360

    steps:
      - name: Free Disk Space (Windows)
        run: |
          Get-PSDrive
          # Remove large unnecessary components
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files (x86)\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\CodeQL" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Ruby" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Go" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Get-PSDrive

      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          path: S

      # MSVC + Ninja can exceed Win32 CreateProcess limits when paths repeat
      # (GitHub Actions default workspace is deep). SUBST yields short W:\ paths
      # embedded in VTK/Slicer ninja rules alongside CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE.
      - name: Map short workspace drive
        shell: pwsh
        run: subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE

      - name: Set up MSVC
        uses: ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd@v1

      - name: Install Qt
        uses: jurplel/install-qt-action@v3
        with:
          version: "5.15.2"
          arch: "win64_msvc2019_64"
          modules: "qtcharts qtwebengine"

      - name: Debug Environment
        run: |
          dir env:
          echo "Qt5_Dir is: $env:Qt5_Dir"

      - name: Install Ninja
        run: choco install ninja

      - name: Configure SuperBuild
        run: |
          cmake -S W:\S -B W:\B `
            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON `
            -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON `
            -DSlicer_USE_SYSTEM_QT:BOOL=ON `
            -DCMAKE_GENERATOR=Ninja `
            -DCMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL=ON `
            -DSlicer_SKIP_ROOT_DIR_MAX_LENGTH_CHECK:BOOL=ON `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_BRAINSTOOLS:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeExplorer:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeImporter:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_CompareVolumes:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_LandmarkRegistration:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_SurfaceToolbox:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_SimpleFilters:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_USE_SimpleITK:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_USE_SCIPY:BOOL=OFF `
            -DQt5_DIR="$env:Qt5_Dir"

      - name: Build SuperBuild
        run: cmake --build W:\B --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS

      - name: Build package (EXE)
        run: |
          cmake --build W:\B\Slicer-build --target package --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS

      - name: Find Installer
        id: find-installer
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $installer = Get-ChildItem -Path W:\B\Slicer-build -Filter "Slicer-*.exe" | Select-Object -First 1
          if ($installer) {
            echo "path=$($installer.FullName)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "name=$($installer.Name)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
          } else {
            Write-Error "Installer not found"
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Upload EXE artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.name }}
          path: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.path }}
          retention-days: 7

31	#.rst:
32	# Global Variables
33	# ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
34	
35	#.rst:
36	# .. cmake:variable:: EXTERNAL_PROJECT_DIR
37	#
38	# This variable describes the directory in which external project files
39	# matching ``<EXTERNAL_PROJECT_FILE_PREFIX><projectname>.cmake`` expression are globbed.
40	#
41	if(NOT EXISTS "${EXTERNAL_PROJECT_DIR}")
42	  set(EXTERNAL_PROJECT_DIR ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/SuperBuild)
43	endif()
44	
45	#.rst:
46	# .. cmake:variable:: EXTERNAL_PROJECT_ADDITIONAL_DIR
47	#
48	# If set, this variable represents an other directory in which external project files
49	# are searched for if not already found in ``EXTERNAL_PROJECT_DIR``.
50	
51	#.rst:
52	# .. cmake:variable:: EXTERNAL_PROJECT_ADDITIONAL_DIRS
53	#
54	# If set, this variable represents additional directories in which external project files
55	# are searched for if not already found in ``EXTERNAL_PROJECT_DIR`` and
56	# ``EXTERNAL_PROJECT_ADDITIONAL_DIR``.
57	
58	#.rst:
59	# .. cmake:variable:: EXTERNAL_PROJECT_FILE_PREFIX
60	#
61	# This variable describes the prefix of the external project files looked up in
62	# ``EXTERNAL_PROJECT_DIR``. It defaults to ``External_``.
63	#
64	if(NOT DEFINED EXTERNAL_PROJECT_FILE_PREFIX)
65	  set(EXTERNAL_PROJECT_FILE_PREFIX "External_")
66	endif()
67	
68	#.rst:
69	# .. cmake:variable:: SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT
70	#
71	# This variable can be set to explicitly identify the name of the top-level project.
72	# If not set, it default to the value of ``CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME``.
73	if(NOT DEFINED SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT)
74	  if(NOT DEFINED CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME)
75	    message(FATAL_ERROR "Failed to initialize variable SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT. Variable CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME is not defined.")
76	  endif()
77	  set(SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT ${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME})
78	endif()
79	
80	#.rst:
81	# .. cmake:variable:: EP_LIST_SEPARATOR
82	#
83	# This variable is used to separate list items when passed in various external project
84	# ``..._COMMAND`` options.
85	#
86	# If defaults to ``^^``.
87	if(NOT DEFINED EP_LIST_SEPARATOR)
88	  set(EP_LIST_SEPARATOR "^^")
89	endif()
90	
91	
92	#.rst:
93	# .. cmake:variable:: EP_GIT_PROTOCOL
94	#
95	# The value of this variable is always set to ``https``.
96	#
97	# Following the removal of git protocol by GitHub, the option
98	# ``<SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT>_USE_GIT_PROTOCOL`` is obsolete.
99	# It allowed to toggle between ``git`` and ``https``.
100	# If this option is enabled, a warning is reported and the option is forced to ``OFF``.
101	#
102	# Similarly, if the variable ``EP_GIT_PROTOCOL`` is already set to ``git``, a warning is reported
103	# and the value is forced to ``https``.
104	#
105	# See details at https://github.blog/2021-09-01-improving-git-protocol-security-github
106	#
107	# The variable ``EP_GIT_PROTOCOL`` can be used when adding external project. For example:
108	#
109	# .. code-block:: cmake
110	#
111	#   ExternalProject_Add(${proj}
112	#     ${${proj}_EP_ARGS}
113	#     GIT_REPOSITORY "${EP_GIT_PROTOCOL}://github.com/Foo/Foo.git"
114	#     [...]
115	#     )
116	#
117	if(DEFINED ${SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT}_USE_GIT_PROTOCOL AND ${SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT}_USE_GIT_PROTOCOL)
118	  message(WARNING "Forcing ${SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT}_USE_GIT_PROTOCOL to OFF (Already set to ON in current scope)")
119	  set(${SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT}_USE_GIT_PROTOCOL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
120	endif()
121	if(DEFINED EP_GIT_PROTOCOL)
122	  if("${EP_GIT_PROTOCOL}" STREQUAL "git")
123	    get_property(_value_set_in_cache CACHE EP_GIT_PROTOCOL PROPERTY VALUE SET)
124	    if(_value_set_in_cache)
125	      message(WARNING "Forcing EP_GIT_PROTOCOL cache variable to 'https' (Already set to '${EP_GIT_PROTOCOL}' in current scope)")
126	      set(EP_GIT_PROTOCOL "https" CACHE STRING "" FORCE)
127	    else()
128	      message(WARNING "Forcing EP_GIT_PROTOCOL variable to 'https' (Already set to '${EP_GIT_PROTOCOL}' in current scope)")
129	      set(EP_GIT_PROTOCOL "https")
130	    endif()
131	  endif()
132	else()
133	  set(EP_GIT_PROTOCOL "https")
134	endif()
135	
136	# Compute -G arg for configuring external projects with the same CMake generator:
137	if(CMAKE_EXTRA_GENERATOR)
138	  set(EP_CMAKE_GENERATOR "${CMAKE_EXTRA_GENERATOR} - ${CMAKE_GENERATOR}")
139	else()
140	  set(EP_CMAKE_GENERATOR "${CMAKE_GENERATOR}")
141	endif()
142	set(EP_CMAKE_GENERATOR_PLATFORM "${CMAKE_GENERATOR_PLATFORM}")
143	set(EP_CMAKE_GENERATOR_TOOLSET "${CMAKE_GENERATOR_TOOLSET}")
144	
145	#.rst:
146	# Functions
147	# ^^^^^^^^^
148	
149	#.rst:
150	# .. cmake:function:: mark_as_superbuild
151	#
152	# .. code-block:: cmake
153	#
154	#  mark_as_superbuild(<varname1>[:<vartype1>] [<varname2>[:<vartype2>] [...]])
155	#
156	# .. code-block:: cmake
157	#
158	#  mark_as_superbuild(
159	#      VARS <varname1>[:<vartype1>] [<varname2>[:<vartype2>] [...]]
160	#      [PROJECTS <projectname> [<projectname> [...]] | ALL_PROJECTS]
161	#      [LABELS <label1> [<label2> [...]]]
162	#    )
163	#
164	# .. code-block:: cmake
165	#
166	#  PROJECTS corresponds to a list of <projectname> that will be added using 'ExternalProject_Add' function.
167	#           If not specified and called within a project file, it defaults to the value of 'SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT'.
168	#           If instead 'ALL_PROJECTS' is specified, the variables and labels will be passed to all projects.
169	#
170	#  VARS is an expected list of variables specified as <varname>:<vartype> to pass to <projectname>
171	#
172	#
173	#  LABELS is an optional list of label to associate with the variable names specified using 'VARS' and passed to
174	#         the <projectname> as CMake CACHE args of the form:
175	#           -D<projectname>_EP_LABEL_<label1>=<varname1>;<varname2>[...]
176	#           -D<projectname>_EP_LABEL_<label2>=<varname1>;<varname2>[...]
177	#
178	function(mark_as_superbuild)
179	  set(options ALL_PROJECTS CMAKE_CMD)
180	  set(oneValueArgs)
181	  set(multiValueArgs VARS PROJECTS LABELS)
182	  cmake_parse_arguments(_sb "${options}" "${oneValueArgs}" "${multiValueArgs}" ${ARGN})
183	
184	  set(_vars ${_sb_UNPARSED_ARGUMENTS})
185	
186	  set(_named_parameters_expected 0)
187	  if(_sb_PROJECTS OR _sb_ALL_PROJECTS  OR _sb_LABELS OR _sb_VARS)
188	    set(_named_parameters_expected 1)
189	    set(_vars ${_sb_VARS})
190	  endif()
191	
192	  if(_named_parameters_expected AND _sb_UNPARSED_ARGUMENTS)
193	    message(FATAL_ERROR "Arguments '${_sb_UNPARSED_ARGUMENTS}' should be associated with VARS parameter !")
194	  endif()
195	
196	  if(_sb_PROJECTS AND _sb_ALL_PROJECTS)
197	    message(FATAL_ERROR "Arguments 'PROJECTS' and 'ALL_PROJECTS' are mutually exclusive !")
198	  endif()
199	
200	  foreach(var ${_vars})
201	    set(_type_specified 0)
202	    if(var MATCHES ":")
203	      set(_type_specified 1)
204	    endif()
205	    # XXX Display warning with variable type is also specified for cache variable.
206	    set(_var ${var})
207	    if(NOT _type_specified)
208	      get_property(_type_set_in_cache CACHE ${_var} PROPERTY TYPE SET)
209	      set(_var_name ${_var})
210	      set(_var_type "STRING")
211	      if(_type_set_in_cache)
212	        get_property(_var_type CACHE ${_var_name} PROPERTY TYPE)
213	      endif()
214	      set(_var ${_var_name}:${_var_type})
215	    endif()
216	    list(APPEND _vars_with_type ${_var})
217	  endforeach()
218	
219	  if(_sb_ALL_PROJECTS)
220	    set(optional_arg_ALL_PROJECTS "ALL_PROJECTS")
221	  else()
222	    set(optional_arg_ALL_PROJECTS PROJECTS ${_sb_PROJECTS})
223	  endif()
224	
225	  _sb_append_to_cmake_args(
226	    VARS ${_vars_with_type} LABELS ${_sb_LABELS} ${optional_arg_ALL_PROJECTS})
227	endfunction()
228	
229	#
230	#  _sb_extract_varname_and_vartype(<cmake_varname_and_type> <varname_var> [<vartype_var>])
231	#
232	#  <cmake_varname_and_type> corresponds to variable name and variable type passed as "<varname>:<vartype>"
233	#
234	#  <varname_var> will be set to "<varname>"
235	#
236	#  <vartype_var> is an optional variable name that will be set to "<vartype>"
237	#
238	function(_sb_extract_varname_and_vartype cmake_varname_and_type varname_var)
239	  set(_vartype_var "${ARGV2}")
240	  string(REPLACE ":" ";" varname_and_vartype ${cmake_varname_and_type})
241	  list(GET varname_and_vartype 0 _varname)
242	  list(GET varname_and_vartype 1 _vartype)
243	  set(${varname_var} ${_varname} PARENT_SCOPE)
244	  if(_vartype_var MATCHES ".+")
245	    set(${_vartype_var} ${_vartype} PARENT_SCOPE)
246	  endif()
247	endfunction()
248	
249	
250	function(_sb_list_to_string separator input_list output_string_var)
251	  set(_string "")
252	  # Get list length
253	  list(LENGTH input_list list_length)
254	  # If the list has 0 or 1 element, there is no need to loop over.
255	  if(list_length LESS 2)
256	    set(_string  "${input_list}")
257	  else()
258	    math(EXPR last_element_index "${list_length} - 1")
259	    foreach(index RANGE ${last_element_index})
260	      # Get current item_value
261	      list(GET input_list ${index} item_value)
262	      if(NOT item_value STREQUAL "")
263	        # .. and append non-empty value to output string
264	        set(_string  "${_string}${item_value}")
265	        # Append separator if current element is NOT the last one.
266	        if(NOT index EQUAL last_element_index)
267	          set(_string  "${_string}${separator}")
268	        endif()
269	      endif()
270	    endforeach()
271	  endif()
272	  set(${output_string_var} ${_string} PARENT_SCOPE)
273	endfunction()
274	
275	#
276	#  _sb_cmakevar_to_cmakearg(<cmake_varname_and_type> <cmake_arg_var> <has_cfg_intdir_var> [<varname_var> [<vartype_var>]])
277	#
278	#  <cmake_varname_and_type> corresponds to variable name and variable type passed as "<varname>:<vartype>"
279	#
280	#  <cmake_arg_var> is a variable name that will be set to "-D<varname>:<vartype>=${<varname>}"
281	#
282	#  <has_int_dir_var> is set to either TRUE or FALSE.
283	#                      FALSE means that the value does NOT reference ${CMAKE_CFG_INTDIR} and
284	#                      the generated cmake argument should be passed to ExternalProject_Add as CMAKE_CACHE_ARGS.
285	#                      TRUEmeans that the value does reference ${CMAKE_CFG_INTDIR} and
286	#                      the generated cmake argument should be passed to ExternalProject_Add as CMAKE_ARGS.
287	#
288	#  <varname_var> is an optional variable name that will be set to "<varname>"
289	#
290	#  <vartype_var> is an optional variable name that will be set to "<vartype>"
291	#
292	function(_sb_cmakevar_to_cmakearg cmake_varname_and_type cmake_arg_var has_cfg_intdir_var)
293	  set(_varname_var "${ARGV3}")
294	  set(_vartype_var "${ARGV4}")
295	
296	  _sb_extract_varname_and_vartype(${cmake_varname_and_type} _varname _vartype)
297	
298	  set(_var_value "${${_varname}}")
299	
300	  # Use cache value unless it is INTERNAL
301	  if(_vartype STREQUAL "INTERNAL")
302	    set(_vartype "STRING")
303	  else()
304	    get_property(_value_set_in_cache CACHE ${_varname} PROPERTY VALUE SET)
305	    if(_value_set_in_cache)
306	      get_property(_var_value CACHE ${_varname} PROPERTY VALUE)
307	    endif()
308	  endif()
309	
310	  set(_has_cfg_intdir FALSE)
311	  if(CMAKE_CONFIGURATION_TYPES)
312	    string(FIND "${_var_value}" ${CMAKE_CFG_INTDIR} _index)
313	    if(NOT _index EQUAL -1)
314	      # Separate list item with <EP_LIST_SEPARATOR>
315	      _sb_list_to_string(${EP_LIST_SEPARATOR} "${_var_value}" _var_value)
316	      set(_has_cfg_intdir TRUE)
317	    endif()
318	  endif()
319	
320	  if(NOT _has_cfg_intdir)
321	    string(REPLACE "\"" "\\\"" _var_value "${_var_value}")
322	  endif()
323	
324	  set(${cmake_arg_var} -D${_varname}:${_vartype}=${_var_value} PARENT_SCOPE)
325	  set(${has_cfg_intdir_var} ${_has_cfg_intdir} PARENT_SCOPE)
326	
327	  if(_varname_var MATCHES ".+")
328	    set(${_varname_var} ${_varname} PARENT_SCOPE)
329	  endif()
330	  if(_vartype_var MATCHES ".+")
331	    set(${_vartype_var} ${_vartype} PARENT_SCOPE)
332	  endif()
333	endfunction()
334	
335	set(_ALL_PROJECT_IDENTIFIER "ALLALLALL")
336	
337	#
338	#  _sb_append_to_cmake_args(
339	#      [VARS <varname1>:<vartype1> [<varname2>:<vartype2> [...]]]
340	#      [PROJECTS <projectname> [<projectname> [...]] | ALL_PROJECTS]
341	#      [LABELS <label1> [<label2> [...]]]
342	#    )
343	#
344	#  PROJECTS corresponds to a list of <projectname> that will be added using 'ExternalProject_Add' function.
345	#           If not specified and called within a project file, it defaults to the value of 'SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT'.
346	#           If instead 'ALL_PROJECTS' is specified, the variables and labels will be passed to all projects.
347	#
348	#  VARS is an expected list of variables specified as <varname>:<vartype> to pass to <projectname>
349	#
350	#
351	#  LABELS is an optional list of label to associate with the variable names specified using 'VARS' and passed to
352	#         the <projectname> as CMake CACHE args of the form:
353	#           -D<projectname>_EP_LABEL_<label1>=<varname1>;<varname2>[...]
354	#           -D<projectname>_EP_LABEL_<label2>=<varname1>;<varname2>[...]
355	#
356	function(_sb_append_to_cmake_args)
357	  set(options ALL_PROJECTS)
358	  set(oneValueArgs)
359	  set(multiValueArgs VARS PROJECTS LABELS)
360	  cmake_parse_arguments(_sb "${options}" "${oneValueArgs}" "${multiValueArgs}" ${ARGN})
361	
362	  if(NOT _sb_PROJECTS AND NOT _sb_ALL_PROJECTS)
363	    set(_sb_PROJECTS ${SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT})
364	  endif()
365	
366	  if(_sb_ALL_PROJECTS)
367	    set(_sb_PROJECTS ${_ALL_PROJECT_IDENTIFIER})
368	  endif()
369	
370	  foreach(_sb_PROJECT ${_sb_PROJECTS})
371	
372	    set(_ep_varnames "")
373	    foreach(varname_and_vartype ${_sb_VARS})
374	      if(NOT TARGET ${_sb_PROJECT})
375	        set_property(GLOBAL APPEND PROPERTY ${_sb_PROJECT}_EP_CMAKE_ARGS ${varname_and_vartype})
376	        _sb_extract_varname_and_vartype(${varname_and_vartype} _varname)
377	      else()
378	        message(FATAL_ERROR "Function _sb_append_to_cmake_args not allowed because project '${_sb_PROJECT}' already added !")
379	      endif()
380	      list(APPEND _ep_varnames ${_varname})
381	    endforeach()
382	
383	    if(_sb_LABELS)
384	      set_property(GLOBAL APPEND PROPERTY ${_sb_PROJECT}_EP_LABELS ${_sb_LABELS})
385	      foreach(label ${_sb_LABELS})
386	        set_property(GLOBAL APPEND PROPERTY ${_sb_PROJECT}_EP_LABEL_${label} ${_ep_varnames})
387	      endforeach()
388	    endif()
389	  endforeach()
390	endfunction()
391	
392	#.rst:
393	# .. cmake:function:: ExternalProject_DeclareLabels
394	#
395	# .. code-block:: cmake
396	#
397	#  ExternalProject_DeclareLabels(
398	#      [PROJECTS <projectname> [<projectname> [...]] | ALL_PROJECTS]
399	#      LABELS <label1> [<label2> [...]]
400	#    )
401	#
402	# .. code-block:: cmake
403	#
404	#  PROJECTS corresponds to a list of <projectname> that will be added using 'ExternalProject_Add' function.
405	#           If not specified and called within a project file, it defaults to the value of 'SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT'.
406	#           If instead 'ALL_PROJECTS' is specified, the variables and labels will be passed to all projects.
407	#
408	#  LABELS is a list of label to pass to the <projectname> as CMake CACHE args of the
409	#           form -D<projectname>_EP_LABEL_<label>= unless specific variables
410	#           have been associated with the labels using mark_as_superbuild.
411	#
412	function(ExternalProject_DeclareLabels)
413	  set(options ALL_PROJECTS)
414	  set(oneValueArgs)
415	  set(multiValueArgs PROJECTS LABELS)
416	  cmake_parse_arguments(_sb "${options}" "${oneValueArgs}" "${multiValueArgs}" ${ARGN})
417	
418	  if(_sb_PROJECTS AND _sb_ALL_PROJECTS)
419	    message(FATAL_ERROR "Arguments 'PROJECTS' and 'ALL_PROJECTS' are mutually exclusive !")
420	  endif()
421	
422	  if(_sb_ALL_PROJECTS)
423	    set(optional_arg_ALL_PROJECTS "ALL_PROJECTS")
424	  else()
425	    set(optional_arg_ALL_PROJECTS PROJECTS ${_sb_PROJECTS})
426	  endif()
427	
428	  _sb_append_to_cmake_args(
429	    LABELS ${_sb_LABELS} ${optional_arg_ALL_PROJECTS})
430	endfunction()
431	
432	function(_sb_get_external_project_arguments proj varname)
433	
434	  mark_as_superbuild(${SUPERBUILD_TOPLEVEL_PROJECT}_USE_SYSTEM_${proj}:BOOL)
435	
436	  function(_sb_collect_args proj)
437	    # Set list of CMake args associated with each label
438	    get_property(_labels GLOBAL PROPERTY ${proj}_EP_LABELS)
439	    if(_labels)
440	      list(REMOVE_DUPLICATES _labels)
441	      foreach(label ${_labels})
442	        get_property(${proj}_EP_LABEL_${label} GLOBAL PROPERTY ${proj}_EP_LABEL_${label})
443	        if(${proj}_EP_LABEL_${label})
444	          list(REMOVE_DUPLICATES ${proj}_EP_LABEL_${label})
445	        endif()
446	        _sb_append_to_cmake_args(PROJECTS ${proj}
447	          VARS ${proj}_EP_LABEL_${label}:STRING)
448	      endforeach()
449	    endif()
450	
451	    get_property(_args GLOBAL PROPERTY ${proj}_EP_CMAKE_ARGS)
452	    foreach(var ${_args})
453	      _sb_cmakevar_to_cmakearg(${var} cmake_arg _has_cfg_intdir)
454	      set(_ep_property "CMAKE_CACHE_ARGS")
455	      if(_has_cfg_intdir)
456	        set(_ep_property "CMAKE_ARGS")
457	      endif()
458	      set_property(GLOBAL APPEND PROPERTY ${proj}_EP_PROPERTY_${_ep_property} ${cmake_arg})
459	    endforeach()
460	
461	  endfunction()
462	
463	  _sb_collect_args(${proj})
464	  _sb_collect_args(${_ALL_PROJECT_IDENTIFIER})
465	
466	  set(_ep_arguments "")
467	
468	  # Option CMAKE_FIND_USE_PACKAGE_REGISTRY was introduced in CMake 3.16
469	  if(NOT CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_LESS "3.16")
470	    if(NOT DEFINED CMAKE_FIND_USE_PACKAGE_REGISTRY)
471	      set(CMAKE_FIND_USE_PACKAGE_REGISTRY OFF)
472	    endif()
473	  else()
474	    if(NOT DEFINED CMAKE_FIND_PACKAGE_NO_PACKAGE_REGISTRY)
475	      set(CMAKE_FIND_PACKAGE_NO_PACKAGE_REGISTRY ON)
476	    endif()
477	  endif()
478	
479	  # Set list of CMake options to propagate
480	  set(_options
481	    CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS:BOOL
482	    CMAKE_FIND_PACKAGE_NO_PACKAGE_REGISTRY:BOOL
483	    CMAKE_JOB_POOL_COMPILE:STRING
484	    CMAKE_JOB_POOL_LINK:STRING
485	    CMAKE_JOB_POOLS:STRING
486	    )
487	  if(NOT CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_LESS "3.16")
488	    list(APPEND _options
489	      CMAKE_FIND_USE_PACKAGE_REGISTRY:BOOL
490	      )
491	  endif()
492	  # Ninja + MSVC embeds paths in very long compiler command lines when using Kits;
493	  # propagating this to ExternalProject configures (VTK, inner Slicer, etc.) avoids
494	  # Windows CreateProcess "~8191 char" failures. See CMake 3.24+.
495	  if(CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_EQUAL "3.24" OR CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_GREATER "3.24")
496	    list(APPEND _options CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL)
497	  endif()
498	
499	  # Automatically propagate CMake options
500	  foreach(_cmake_option_and_type IN LISTS _options)
501	    _sb_extract_varname_and_vartype(${_cmake_option_and_type} _cmake_option _cmake_option_type)
502	    if(DEFINED ${_cmake_option})
503	      list(APPEND _ep_arguments CMAKE_CACHE_ARGS
504	        -D${_cmake_option}:${_cmake_option_type}=${${_cmake_option}}
505	        )
506	    endif()
507	  endforeach()
508	
509	  foreach(property CMAKE_ARGS CMAKE_CACHE_ARGS)
510	    get_property(${proj}_EP_PROPERTY_${property} GLOBAL PROPERTY ${proj}_EP_PROPERTY_${property})
511	    get_property(${_ALL_PROJECT_IDENTIFIER}_EP_PROPERTY_${property} GLOBAL PROPERTY ${_ALL_PROJECT_IDENTIFIER}_EP_PROPERTY_${property})
512	    set(_all ${${proj}_EP_PROPERTY_${property}} ${${_ALL_PROJECT_IDENTIFIER}_EP_PROPERTY_${property}})
513	    list(LENGTH _all _num_properties)
514	    if(_num_properties GREATER 0)
515	      list(APPEND _ep_arguments ${property} ${_all})
516	    endif()
517	  endforeach()
518	
519	  list(APPEND _ep_arguments LIST_SEPARATOR ${EP_LIST_SEPARATOR})
520	
521	  list(APPEND _ep_arguments CMAKE_GENERATOR ${_sb_CMAKE_GENERATOR})
522	  if(CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_GREATER "3.0")
523	    list(APPEND _ep_arguments CMAKE_GENERATOR_PLATFORM ${_sb_CMAKE_GENERATOR_PLATFORM})
524	  endif()
525	  list(APPEND _ep_arguments CMAKE_GENERATOR_TOOLSET ${_sb_CMAKE_GENERATOR_TOOLSET})
526	  if(CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_EQUAL "3.4" OR CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_GREATER "3.4")
527	    # USES_TERMINAL_* options were introduced in CMake 3.4
528	    foreach(step IN ITEMS DOWNLOAD UPDATE CONFIGURE BUILD TEST INSTALL)
529	      list(APPEND _ep_arguments
530	        USES_TERMINAL_${step} 1
531	        )
532	    endforeach()
533	  endif()
534	  if(CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_EQUAL "3.24" OR CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_GREATER "3.24")
535	    # See https://cmake.org/cmake/help/latest/policy/CMP0135.html
536	    list(APPEND _ep_arguments DOWNLOAD_EXTRACT_TIMESTAMP 1)
537	  endif()
538	  if(CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_EQUAL "3.28" OR CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_GREATER "3.28")
539	    # Address "gmake[N]: *** read jobs pipe: Bad file descriptor." issue associated with
540	    # build command.
541	    # For more details:
542	    # - https://gitlab.kitware.com/cmake/cmake/-/issues/26398
543	    # - https://gitlab.kitware.com/cmake/cmake/-/merge_requests/8667
544	    # - https://gitlab.kitware.com/cmake/cmake/-/merge_requests/10014
545	    list(APPEND _ep_arguments BUILD_JOB_SERVER_AWARE ON)
546	  endif()
547	  set(${varname} ${_ep_arguments} PARENT_SCOPE)
548	endfunction()
549	
550	function(_sb_update_indent proj)
551	  superbuild_stack_size(SB_PROJECT_STACK _stack_size)
552	  set(_indent "")
553	  if(_stack_size GREATER 0)
554	    foreach(not_used RANGE 1 ${_stack_size})
555	      set(_indent "  ${_indent}")
556	    endforeach()
557	  endif()
558	  set_property(GLOBAL PROPERTY SUPERBUILD_${proj}_INDENT ${_indent})
559	endfunction()
560	
561	#.rst:
562	# .. cmake:function:: ExternalProject_Message
563	#
564	# .. code-block:: cmake
565	#
566	#  ExternalProject_Message(<project_name> <msg> [condition])
567	#
568	function(ExternalProject_Message proj msg)
569	  set(_display 1)
570	  if(NOT "x${ARGV2}" STREQUAL "x")
571	    set(_display ${ARGN})
572	  endif()
573	  if(${_display})
574	    get_property(_indent GLOBAL PROPERTY SUPERBUILD_${proj}_INDENT)
575	    message(STATUS "SuperBuild - ${_indent}${msg}")
576	  endif()
577	endfunction()
578	
579	#
580	#  superbuild_stack_content(<stack_name> <output_var>)
581	#
582	#  <stack_name> corresponds to the name of stack.
583	#
584	#  <output_var> is the name of CMake variable that will be set with the content
585	#  of the stack identified by <stack_name>.
586	#
587	function(superbuild_stack_content stack_name output_var)
588	  get_property(_stack GLOBAL PROPERTY ${stack_name})
589	  set(${output_var} ${_stack} PARENT_SCOPE)
590	endfunction()
591	
592	#
593	#  superbuild_stack_size(<stack_name> <output_var>)
594	#
595	#  <stack_name> corresponds to the name of stack.
596	#
597	#  <output_var> is the name of CMake variable that will be set with the size
598	#  of the stack identified by <stack_name>.
599	#
600	function(superbuild_stack_size stack_name output_var)
601	  get_property(_stack GLOBAL PROPERTY ${stack_name})
602	  list(LENGTH _stack _stack_size)
603	  set(${output_var} ${_stack_size} PARENT_SCOPE)
604	endfunction()
605	
606	#
607	#  superbuild_stack_push(<stack_name> <value>)
608	#
609	#  <stack_name> corresponds to the name of stack.
610	#
611	#  <value> is appended to the stack identified by <stack_name>.
612	#
613	function(superbuild_stack_push stack_name value)
614	  set_property(GLOBAL APPEND PROPERTY ${stack_name} ${value})
615	endfunction()
616	
617	#
618	#  superbuild_stack_pop(<stack_name> <item_var>)
619	#
620	#  <stack_name> corresponds to the name of stack.
621	#
622	#  <item_var> names a CMake variable that will be set with the item
623	#  removed from the stack identified by <stack_name>
624	#
625	function(superbuild_stack_pop stack_name item_var)
626	  get_property(_stack GLOBAL PROPERTY ${stack_name})
627	  list(LENGTH _stack _stack_size)
628	  if(_stack_size GREATER 0)
629	    math(EXPR _index_to_remove "${_stack_size} - 1")
630	    list(GET _stack ${_index_to_remove} _item)
631	    list(REMOVE_AT _stack ${_index_to_remove})
632	    set_property(GLOBAL PROPERTY ${stack_name} ${_stack})
633	    set(${item_var} ${_item} PARENT_SCOPE)
634	  endif()
635	endfunction()
636	
637	function(_sb_is_optional proj output_var)
638	  set(_include_project 1)
639	  if(COMMAND superbuild_is_external_project_includable)
640	    superbuild_is_external_project_includable("${proj}" _include_project)
641	  endif()
642	  set(optional 1)
643	  if(_include_project)
644	    set(optional 0)
645	  endif()
646	  set(${output_var} ${optional} PARENT_SCOPE)
647	endfunction()
