diff --git a/.github/workflows/build-windows.yml b/.github/workflows/build-windows.yml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..4adab25bb5
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.github/workflows/build-windows.yml
@@ -0,0 +1,113 @@
+name: Build Windows EXE
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches:
+      - main
+      - design
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+jobs:
+  build-windows:
+    name: Build Windows EXE
+    runs-on: windows-2022
+    timeout-minutes: 360
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Free Disk Space (Windows)
+        run: |
+          Get-PSDrive
+          # Remove large unnecessary components
+          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files (x86)\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\CodeQL" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Ruby" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Go" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+          Get-PSDrive
+
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          path: S
+
+      # MSVC + Ninja can exceed Win32 CreateProcess limits when paths repeat
+      # (GitHub Actions default workspace is deep). SUBST yields short W:\ paths
+      # embedded in VTK/Slicer ninja rules alongside CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE.
+      - name: Map short workspace drive
+        shell: pwsh
+        run: subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE
+
+      - name: Setup short TEMP path
+        shell: pwsh
+        run: |
+          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path W:\T -Force
+          "TEMP=W:\T" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append
+          "TMP=W:\T"  | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append
+
+      - name: Set up MSVC
+        uses: ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd@v1
+
+      - name: Install Qt
+        uses: jurplel/install-qt-action@v3
+        with:
+          version: "5.15.2"
+          arch: "win64_msvc2019_64"
+          modules: "qtcharts qtwebengine"
+
+      - name: Debug Environment
+        run: |
+          dir env:
+          echo "Qt5_Dir is: $env:Qt5_Dir"
+
+      - name: Install Ninja
+        run: choco install ninja
+
+      - name: Configure SuperBuild
+        run: |
+          cmake -G Ninja -S W:\S -B W:\B `
+            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON `
+            -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON `
+            -DSlicer_USE_SYSTEM_QT:BOOL=ON `
+            -DCMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL=ON `
+            -DSlicer_SKIP_ROOT_DIR_MAX_LENGTH_CHECK:BOOL=ON `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_BRAINSTOOLS:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeExplorer:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeImporter:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_CompareVolumes:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_LandmarkRegistration:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_SurfaceToolbox:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_BUILD_SimpleFilters:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_USE_SimpleITK:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DSlicer_USE_SCIPY:BOOL=OFF `
+            -DQt5_DIR="$env:Qt5_Dir"
+
+      - name: Build SuperBuild
+        run: cmake --build W:\B --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS
+
+      - name: Build package (EXE)
+        run: |
+          cmake --build W:\B\Slicer-build --target package --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS
+

       - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
 
+      - uses: actions/create-github-app-token@f8d387b68d61c58ab83c6c016672934102569859 # v3.0.0

file.content:
name: Update Slicer.crt certificate bundle

on:
  schedule:
    # Every tuesday at 11.30am UTC (7.30am ET)
    - cron: "30 11 * * 2"
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  update-slicer-certificate-bundle:
    name: Update Slicer.crt certificate bundle
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      CERTDATA_OWNER: mozilla
      CERTDATA_REPO: gecko-dev
      CERTDATA_PATH: security/nss/lib/ckfw/builtins/certdata.txt
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: actions/create-github-app-token@f8d387b68d61c58ab83c6c016672934102569859 # v3.0.0
        id: app-token
        continue-on-error: true
        with:
          app-id: ${{ vars.SLICER_APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ secrets.SLICER_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}

      - name: Set token
        id: set-token
        run: |
          if [ "${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}" != "" ]; then
            echo "token=${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "token=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Get sha and canonical download url for latest version of certdata.txt file
        id: latest_certdata
        run: |
          sha=$(curl -s -H "Authorization: token ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ env.CERTDATA_OWNER }}/${{ env.CERTDATA_REPO }}/commits?path=${{ env.CERTDATA_PATH }}&sha=master&per_page=1" | jq ".[0] | .sha" -r)
          echo "sha=${sha}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          download_url="https://github.com/${{ env.CERTDATA_OWNER }}/${{ env.CERTDATA_REPO }}/blob/${sha}/${{ env.CERTDATA_PATH }}?raw=true"
          echo "download_url=${download_url}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Download certdata.txt from https://github.com/mozilla/gecko-dev
        run: |
          cd Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs                           &&
          curl -L# -o certdata.txt ${{ steps.latest_certdata.outputs.download_url }}

      - name: Generate Slicer.crt
        run: |
          cd Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs                           &&
          ./make-ca.sh

      - name: Cleanup
        run: |
          cd Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs                           &&
          rm certdata.txt

      - name: Get SHA of the branch that triggered the workflow run
        id: head_branch
        run: |
          sha=$(git rev-parse ${{ github.ref }})
          echo "sha=${sha}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create Pull Request
        id: cpr
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@c0f553fe549906ede9cf27b5156039d195d2ece0 # v8.1.0
        with:
          token: ${{ steps.set-token.outputs.token }}
          commit-message: |
            ENH: Update Slicer.crt CA bundle

            This commit updates Slicer.crt certificate bundle with [make-ca.sh][1] script using the
            content of [certdata][2] associated with ${{ env.CERTDATA_OWNER }}/${{ env.CERTDATA_REPO }}@${{ steps.latest_certdata.outputs.sha }},

            It was auto-generated by the [update-slicer-certificate-bundle][3] GitHub action workflow.

            [1]: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/blob/${{ steps.head_branch.outputs.sha }}/Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs/make-ca.sh
            [2]: ${{ steps.latest_certdata.outputs.download_url }}
            [3]: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/blob/${{ steps.head_branch.outputs.sha }}/.github/workflows/update-slicer-certificate-bundle.yml
          committer: Slicer Bot <slicerbot@slicer.org>
          author: Slicer Bot <slicerbot@slicer.org>
          signoff: false
          branch: slicerbot/update-slicer-certificate-bundle
          delete-branch: true
          title: "Update Slicer.crt CA bundle"
          body: |
            This pull-request was auto-generated by the [update-slicer-certificate-bundle][1] GitHub action workflow.

            [1]: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/blob/${{ steps.head_branch.outputs.sha }}/.github/workflows/update-slicer-certificate-bundle.yml
          draft: false

content.text: Found it. Prettier (and YAML parsers) choke on `subst W: $env:...` — the `W:` is parsed as a YAML mapping key. The `run:` value must be quoted or use block scalar `|`. Let me fix it and check everything else:

        run: subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE

        run: |
          subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Build Windows EXE

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - design
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  build-windows:
    name: Build Windows EXE
    runs-on: windows-2022
    timeout-minutes: 360

    steps:
      - name: Free Disk Space (Windows)
        run: |
          Get-PSDrive
          # Remove large unnecessary components
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files (x86)\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\CodeQL" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Ruby" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Go" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Get-PSDrive

      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          path: S

      # MSVC + Ninja can exceed Win32 CreateProcess limits when paths repeat
      # (GitHub Actions default workspace is deep). SUBST yields short W:\ paths
      # embedded in VTK/Slicer ninja rules alongside CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE.
      - name: Map short workspace drive
        shell: pwsh
        run: subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE

      - name: Setup short TEMP path
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path W:\T -Force
          "TEMP=W:\T" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append
          "TMP=W:\T"  | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append

      - name: Set up MSVC
        uses: ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd@v1

      - name: Install Qt
        uses: jurplel/install-qt-action@v3
        with:
          version: "5.15.2"
          arch: "win64_msvc2019_64"
          modules: "qtcharts qtwebengine"

      - name: Debug Environment
        run: |
          dir env:
          echo "Qt5_Dir is: $env:Qt5_Dir"

      - name: Install Ninja
        run: choco install ninja

      - name: Configure SuperBuild
        run: |
          cmake -G Ninja -S W:\S -B W:\B `
            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON `
            -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON `
            -DSlicer_USE_SYSTEM_QT:BOOL=ON `
            -DCMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL=ON `
            -DSlicer_SKIP_ROOT_DIR_MAX_LENGTH_CHECK:BOOL=ON `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_BRAINSTOOLS:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeExplorer:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeImporter:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_CompareVolumes:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_LandmarkRegistration:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_SurfaceToolbox:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_SimpleFilters:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_USE_SimpleITK:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_USE_SCIPY:BOOL=OFF `
            -DQt5_DIR="$env:Qt5_Dir"

      - name: Build SuperBuild
        run: cmake --build W:\B --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS

      - name: Build package (EXE)
        run: |
          cmake --build W:\B\Slicer-build --target package --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS

      - name: Find Installer
        id: find-installer
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $installer = Get-ChildItem -Path W:\B\Slicer-build -Filter "Slicer-*.exe" | Select-Object -First 1
          if ($installer) {
            echo "path=$($installer.FullName)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "name=$($installer.Name)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
          } else {
            Write-Error "Installer not found"
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Upload EXE artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.name }}
          path: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.path }}
          retention-days: 7

Base directory for this skill: /Users/tanishqsingh/.claude/skills/token-efficient-coding

# Token-Efficient Coding Profile

Source: https://github.com/drona23/claude-token-efficient

## Output Rules
- Return code first. Explanation after, only if non-obvious.
- No inline prose. Use comments sparingly - only where logic is unclear.
- No boilerplate unless explicitly requested.

## Code Rules
- Simplest working solution. No over-engineering.
- No abstractions for single-use operations.
- No speculative features or "you might also want..."
- Read the file before modifying it. Never edit blind.
- No docstrings or type annotations on code not being changed.
- No error handling for scenarios that cannot happen.
- Three similar lines is better than a premature abstraction.

## Review Rules
- State the bug. Show the fix. Stop.
- No suggestions beyond the scope of the review.
- No compliments on the code before or after the review.

## Debugging Rules
- Never speculate about a bug without reading the relevant code first.
- State what you found, where, and the fix. One pass.
- If cause is unclear: say so. Do not guess.

## Formatting
- No em dashes, smart quotes, or decorative Unicode symbols.
- Plain hyphens and straight quotes only.
- Code output must be copy-paste safe.

- Output sounds human. Never AI-generated.
- Never use em-dashes or replacement hyphens.

- Hyphens map to standard grammar only.

- These rules apply to English prose, not code.
- Code output should remain readable and idiomatic.
- Natural language characters (accented letters, CJK, etc.) are fine when content requires them.

# my-mac-claude — Combined Guidelines

## Mandatory Workflow Gates

**Before starting any new project or implementing any new feature:**
1. Run `/grill-me` — interrogate assumptions and requirements
2. Run `/plan-eng-review` — review the engineering plan before writing code

No exceptions. These gates apply to every implementation request.

## Who You Are Working With

Yash Singh — AI-fueled full-stack engineer (SDE at Binocs, Bangalore). Final-year B.Tech CS at IIIT Una, graduating July 2026. See full profile in memory.

## If You Are an AI Agent

This is a personal combined skills library for macOS. When working with this repo:

1. Skills are organized under `skills/` by source collection:
   - `skills/anthropic/` — Anthropic example skills (document processing, design, testing)
   - `skills/superpowers/` — Core development workflows (TDD, debugging, collaboration)
   - `skills/mattpocock/` — Matt Pocock's engineering & productivity skills
   - `skills/gstack/` — gstack browser-testing and team workflow skills
   - `skills/token-efficient/` — Token-efficient response profiles
   - `skills/obsidian-second-brain/` — Obsidian vault AI-first second brain (31 commands)

2. Every skill must have a `SKILL.md` file in its directory.

3. Flat symlinks at `skills/<name>` enable auto-discovery by Claude Code.

4. The top-level `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` is the main manifest. Keep it in sync when adding/removing skills.

5. The `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` organizes skills into logical groups for the marketplace.

## macOS Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/yashs33244/claude-god-setup.git ~/.claude
```

Claude will automatically discover and load all skills from flat symlinks in `~/.claude/skills/`.

## Adding New Skills

1. Create a new directory under the appropriate `skills/` subfolder
2. Add a `SKILL.md` file following the standard format
3. Create a flat symlink: `ln -sfn skills/<collection>/<skill-name> skills/<skill-name>`
4. Register the skill path in both `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` and `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`
5. Update `README.md` with a one-line description

## Remotes

- `origin` → https://github.com/yashs33244/my-mac-claude (skills repo)
- `claude-god-setup` → https://github.com/yashs33244/claude-god-setup (full environment backup)

Auto-commit + push runs on every Claude Code Stop event via hook in `settings.json`.

## License

MIT

input.new_string:
## Peer AI Agents Available

These CLIs are installed and usable via `Bash` tool for delegation or parallel work.

### Google Gemini CLI
- Binary: `/opt/homebrew/bin/gemini`
- Use for: code analysis, lint review, second opinions, large-context reads.
- Invocation: `gemini -p "your prompt"` or pipe files in.
- Runs in background with `run_in_background: true` for parallel tasks.

### OpenAI Codex
- Not currently installed. Check with `which codex` before attempting to use.

### Orchestration pattern
When a task is large or parallelizable, delegate subtasks to Gemini:
```bash
gemini -p "analyze X and return findings" &
# do own work in parallel
wait
```
Read results from the background task output file when done.

## License

MIT

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Fix a Windows CI build failure: Ninja/MSVC `CreateProcess` error ("command line too long") during the Slicer SuperBuild compile step on GitHub Actions (PR #6, job URL ending in `?pr=6`).
   - Fix all linting and CI action failures on the `design` branch.
   - Orchestrate peer AI agents (Gemini CLI, OpenAI Codex) for large or parallelizable tasks.
   - Update global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` to document available peer agents.
   - Create a `CLAUDE.md` for the dicom-stl repo (done via /init at session start).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Windows `CreateProcess` 32,767-char command-line limit hit by Ninja when spawning `cl.exe` with long include/define lists
   - `CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE` (CMake 3.24+) - makes Ninja use `@rspfile` instead of passing all args directly to `CreateProcess`
   - `-G Ninja` vs `-DCMAKE_GENERATOR=Ninja`: only `-G` actually selects the generator; `-DCMAKE_GENERATOR=Ninja` sets a cache variable but CMake ignores it for generator selection on the outer configure
   - `ExternalProjectDependency.cmake` propagation mechanism: `_sb_get_external_project_arguments` collects variables from `_options` list and passes them as `CMAKE_CACHE_ARGS` to all `ExternalProject_Add` sub-builds
   - Windows `subst` virtual drive mapping to shorten workspace paths (`W:\`)
   - Short `TEMP`/`TMP` paths needed so response-file paths themselves stay short
   - `prettier` YAML parser treating `run: subst W: $env:...` as a nested mapping due to `W:` being parsed as a YAML key
   - Pre-commit hooks: `check-yaml`, `trailing-whitespace`, `end-of-file-fixer`, `mixed-line-ending`, `ruff-check`, `clang-format`, `pyupgrade`, `prettier` (yaml), `check-github-workflows`, `check-dependabot`
   - Gemini CLI at `/opt/homebrew/bin/gemini` as a peer agent for delegation

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `.github/workflows/build-windows.yml` (new file, created in this branch)
     - Root cause of TWO bugs: `run: subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE` caused YAML parse failure in prettier; `-DCMAKE_GENERATOR=Ninja` was a cache variable, not a generator selector
     - Fixed generator selection to `-G Ninja` on cmake command directly
     - Fixed YAML parse error by wrapping `subst` in block scalar `|`
     - Added "Setup short TEMP path" step that creates `W:\T` and exports `TEMP=W:\T` and `TMP=W:\T` to `$GITHUB_ENV`
     ```yaml
     - name: Map short workspace drive
       shell: pwsh
       run: |
         subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE

     - name: Setup short TEMP path
       shell: pwsh
       run: |
         New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path W:\T -Force
         "TEMP=W:\T" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append
         "TMP=W:\T"  | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append

     - name: Configure SuperBuild
       run: |
         cmake -G Ninja -S W:\S -B W:\B `
           -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release `
           -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON `
           -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON `
           -DSlicer_USE_SYSTEM_QT:BOOL=ON `
           -DCMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL=ON `
           ...
     ```

   - `CMake/ExternalProjectDependency.cmake`
     - The function `_sb_get_external_project_arguments` builds the `CMAKE_CACHE_ARGS` list passed to every `ExternalProject_Add` sub-build (VTK, ITK, inner Slicer, etc.)
     - Previously had a `CMAKE_VERSION >= 3.24` gate around adding `CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL` to `_options` - removed the gate to make propagation unconditional
     ```cmake
     # Before (version-gated):
     if(CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_EQUAL "3.24" OR CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_GREATER "3.24")
       list(APPEND _options CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL)
     endif()

     # After (unconditional):
     # Ninja + MSVC embeds paths in very long compiler command lines when using Kits;
     # propagating this to ExternalProject configures (VTK, inner Slicer, etc.) avoids
     # Windows CreateProcess "~8191 char" failures. Variable added in CMake 3.24;
     # propagate unconditionally - unknown cache vars are silently ignored on older CMake.
     list(APPEND _options CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL)
     ```

   - `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (global user instructions)
     - Added "Peer AI Agents Available" section documenting Gemini CLI and Codex
     ```markdown
     ## Peer AI Agents Available

     These CLIs are installed and usable via `Bash` tool for delegation or parallel work.

     ### Google Gemini CLI
     - Binary: `/opt/homebrew/bin/gemini`
     - Use for: code analysis, lint review, second opinions, large-context reads.
     - Invocation: `gemini -p "your prompt"` or pipe files in.
     - Runs in background with `run_in_background: true` for parallel tasks.

     ### OpenAI Codex
     - Not currently installed. Check with `which codex` before attempting to use.

     ### Orchestration pattern
     When a task is large or parallelizable, delegate subtasks to Gemini:
     ```bash
     gemini -p "analyze X and return findings" &
     # do own work in parallel
     wait
     ```
     ```

   - `CLAUDE.md` (repo-level, created at session start via /init)
     - Documents build commands, incremental rebuild, packaging, testing, linting, commit format, architecture table, MVP flag behavior, development setup

   - `SuperBuild/External_python.cmake`
     - Added WIN32 guard for `CMAKE_SHARED_LINKER_FLAGS` and `CMAKE_MODULE_LINKER_FLAGS` pointing to `/LIBPATH:${EP_BINARY_DIR}/CMakeBuild/libpython`
     - Uncommented `-DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=OFF`

   - `Base/QTApp/qSlicerMainWindow.cxx`
     - Removed large inline `qApp->setStyleSheet(R"(...)")` block
     - Replaced with loading stylesheet from QRC resource (`Product.qss`)
     - clang-format check confirmed: no formatting changes needed

   - `Base/QTApp/Resources/Style/Product.qss` (new file)
     - External QSS stylesheet extracted from the inline C++ string
     - 151 lines of Qt CSS for the warm-cream design system

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Windows `CreateProcess` error ("command line too long")**:
     - Root causes: (1) `-DCMAKE_GENERATOR=Ninja` doesn't select Ninja for the outer build; (2) `CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE` was version-gated in propagation; (3) no short TEMP path
     - Fixes: `-G Ninja` in configure, unconditional propagation in `ExternalProjectDependency.cmake`, `W:\T` TEMP setup
   - **Prettier YAML parse error on `build-windows.yml` line 41**:
     - `run: subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE` - `W:` parsed as YAML mapping key inside compact mapping
     - Fix: wrapped in block scalar `run: |` form
     - Verified with `npx prettier --check`: both workflow files now pass
   - **Gemini CLI hallucination**: Gemini falsely reported "space before @" in action references. Ignored - direct inspection showed no such spaces existed.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified three independent causes for the Windows CreateProcess failure and applied layered fixes
   - Used `npx prettier --check` locally to pinpoint the exact YAML lint failure - Gemini's analysis was unreliable on this
   - Used `/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang-format` to verify `qSlicerMainWindow.cxx` needed no formatting changes
   - All pre-commit lint checks (prettier, trailing-whitespace, clang-format) now pass locally on changed files

6. All User Messages:
   - /init command to create CLAUDE.md
   - "Actually the Windows build workflow... Not working. You have to fix the Windows build workflow. The job failed during the Slicer (SuperBuild) compile step on Windows because Ninja/Windows couldn't spawn cl.exe... CreateProcess failed. Command attempted: ... cl.exe ... ninja: fatal: CreateProcess: The parameter is incorrect. (is the command line too long?)"
   - "now check"
   - "make sure to fix and push the changes"
   - "The linting and other actions are failing in this branch. What you have to do is just orchestrate the agents like Gemini CLI and Codex from OpenAI, which are already installed in my system... First of all you have to fix it and if it is big work you can delegate those tasks to them and also update these points in my global cloud.md where you can have other agents available with you like OpenAI Codex and Google's Gemini CLI."
   - /token-efficient-coding (skill load)
   - /token-efficient-rules (skill load)
   - Summary request (current message)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Verify Windows build actually passes in CI now that all three fixes are applied (can only be confirmed by GitHub Actions run)
   - Codex is not installed - user mentioned it is available but `which codex` returned not found

8. Current Work:
   All planned fixes are complete and pushed. The last two commits on `design`:
   - `b2e61d993b` - STYLE: Fix YAML parse error in Windows build workflow (prettier fix)
   - `9729fc0596` - COMP: Fix Windows Ninja CreateProcess command-line-too-long failure (generator + response files + TEMP)

   Global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` updated with peer agent documentation.

   Token-efficient coding and rules profiles were loaded just before the summary request.

9. Optional Next Step:
   No explicit pending task remains. The user's most recent explicit request was: "make sure to fix and push the changes" (regarding the linting failures). All identified lint failures are fixed and pushed. The next action would be to wait for CI to confirm the Windows build passes, but that requires no local action. If the CI still fails, investigate the specific new failure from the GitHub Actions logs.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/tanishqsingh/.claude/projects/-Users-tanishqsingh-Desktop-yash-desktop-files-madhav-binocs-dicom-stl/fcf633e9-6935-4cec-b414-4ba053c7ce9c.jsonl

<local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

file.content:
name: Build Windows EXE

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - design
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  build-windows:
    name: Build Windows EXE
    runs-on: windows-2022
    timeout-minutes: 360

    steps:
      - name: Free Disk Space (Windows)
        run: |
          Get-PSDrive
          # Remove large unnecessary components
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\Program Files (x86)\Android" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\CodeQL" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Ruby" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          rm -Recurse -Force "C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\Go" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Get-PSDrive

      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          path: S

      # MSVC + Ninja can exceed Win32 CreateProcess limits when paths repeat
      # (GitHub Actions default workspace is deep). SUBST yields short W:\ paths
      # embedded in VTK/Slicer ninja rules alongside CMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE.
      - name: Map short workspace drive
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          subst W: $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE

      - name: Setup short TEMP path
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path W:\T -Force
          "TEMP=W:\T" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append
          "TMP=W:\T"  | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Encoding utf8 -Append

      - name: Set up MSVC
        uses: ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd@v1

      - name: Install Qt
        uses: jurplel/install-qt-action@v3
        with:
          version: "5.15.2"
          arch: "win64_msvc2019_64"
          modules: "qtcharts qtwebengine"

      - name: Debug Environment
        run: |
          dir env:
          echo "Qt5_Dir is: $env:Qt5_Dir"

      - name: Install Ninja
        run: choco install ninja

      - name: Configure SuperBuild
        run: |
          cmake -G Ninja -S W:\S -B W:\B `
            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_WORKFLOW_MVP=ON `
            -DSlicer_SUPERBUILD=ON `
            -DSlicer_USE_SYSTEM_QT:BOOL=ON `
            -DCMAKE_NINJA_FORCE_RESPONSE_FILE:BOOL=ON `
            -DSlicer_SKIP_ROOT_DIR_MAX_LENGTH_CHECK:BOOL=ON `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_BRAINSTOOLS:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeExplorer:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_MultiVolumeImporter:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_CompareVolumes:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_LandmarkRegistration:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_SurfaceToolbox:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_BUILD_SimpleFilters:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_USE_SimpleITK:BOOL=OFF `
            -DSlicer_USE_SCIPY:BOOL=OFF `
            -DQt5_DIR="$env:Qt5_Dir"

      - name: Build SuperBuild
        run: cmake --build W:\B --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS

      - name: Build package (EXE)
        run: |
          cmake --build W:\B\Slicer-build --target package --parallel $env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS

      - name: Find Installer
        id: find-installer
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $installer = Get-ChildItem -Path W:\B\Slicer-build -Filter "Slicer-*.exe" | Select-Object -First 1
          if ($installer) {
            echo "path=$($installer.FullName)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "name=$($installer.Name)" >> $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT
          } else {
            Write-Error "Installer not found"
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Upload EXE artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.name }}
          path: ${{ steps.find-installer.outputs.path }}
          retention-days: 7

name: Update Slicer.crt certificate bundle

on:
  schedule:
    # Every tuesday at 11.30am UTC (7.30am ET)
    - cron: "30 11 * * 2"
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  update-slicer-certificate-bundle:
    name: Update Slicer.crt certificate bundle
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      CERTDATA_OWNER: mozilla
      CERTDATA_REPO: gecko-dev
      CERTDATA_PATH: security/nss/lib/ckfw/builtins/certdata.txt
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: actions/create-github-app-token@f8d387b68d61c58ab83c6c016672934102569859 # v3.0.0
        id: app-token
        continue-on-error: true
        with:
          app-id: ${{ vars.SLICER_APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ secrets.SLICER_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}

      - name: Set token
        id: set-token
        run: |
          if [ "${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}" != "" ]; then
            echo "token=${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "token=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Get sha and canonical download url for latest version of certdata.txt file
        id: latest_certdata
        run: |
          sha=$(curl -s -H "Authorization: token ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ env.CERTDATA_OWNER }}/${{ env.CERTDATA_REPO }}/commits?path=${{ env.CERTDATA_PATH }}&sha=master&per_page=1" | jq ".[0] | .sha" -r)
          echo "sha=${sha}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          download_url="https://github.com/${{ env.CERTDATA_OWNER }}/${{ env.CERTDATA_REPO }}/blob/${sha}/${{ env.CERTDATA_PATH }}?raw=true"
          echo "download_url=${download_url}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Download certdata.txt from https://github.com/mozilla/gecko-dev
        run: |
          cd Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs                           &&
          curl -L# -o certdata.txt ${{ steps.latest_certdata.outputs.download_url }}

      - name: Generate Slicer.crt
        run: |
          cd Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs                           &&
          ./make-ca.sh

      - name: Cleanup
        run: |
          cd Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs                           &&
          rm certdata.txt

      - name: Get SHA of the branch that triggered the workflow run
        id: head_branch
        run: |
          sha=$(git rev-parse ${{ github.ref }})
          echo "sha=${sha}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create Pull Request
        id: cpr
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@c0f553fe549906ede9cf27b5156039d195d2ece0 # v8.1.0
        with:
          token: ${{ steps.set-token.outputs.token }}
          commit-message: |
            ENH: Update Slicer.crt CA bundle

            This commit updates Slicer.crt certificate bundle with [make-ca.sh][1] script using the
            content of [certdata][2] associated with ${{ env.CERTDATA_OWNER }}/${{ env.CERTDATA_REPO }}@${{ steps.latest_certdata.outputs.sha }},

            It was auto-generated by the [update-slicer-certificate-bundle][3] GitHub action workflow.

            [1]: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/blob/${{ steps.head_branch.outputs.sha }}/Base/QTCore/Resources/Certs/make-ca.sh
            [2]: ${{ steps.latest_certdata.outputs.download_url }}
            [3]: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/blob/${{ steps.head_branch.outputs.sha }}/.github/workflows/update-slicer-certificate-bundle.yml
          committer: Slicer Bot <slicerbot@slicer.org>
          author: Slicer Bot <slicerbot@slicer.org>
          signoff: false
          branch: slicerbot/update-slicer-certificate-bundle
          delete-branch: true
          title: "Update Slicer.crt CA bundle"
          body: |
            This pull-request was auto-generated by the [update-slicer-certificate-bundle][1] GitHub action workflow.

            [1]: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/blob/${{ steps.head_branch.outputs.sha }}/.github/workflows/update-slicer-certificate-bundle.yml
          draft: false

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

**Scope**: QSS stylesheet, splash screen SVG, display name, and description

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?>
<svg
   xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
   xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"
   width="573"
   height="376"
   viewBox="0 0 573 376"
   version="1.1">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bgGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#F1F5F9"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#E0F2FE"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="accentGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="0%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#0EA5E9"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#0369A1"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="layerGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#38BDF8" stop-opacity="0.7"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#0369A1" stop-opacity="0.9"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="shadow" x="-10%" y="-10%" width="120%" height="120%">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="4" stdDeviation="8" flood-color="#0369A1" flood-opacity="0.15"/>
    </filter>
    <filter id="panelShadow" x="-5%" y="-5%" width="110%" height="115%">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="2" stdDeviation="4" flood-color="#0F172A" flood-opacity="0.08"/>
    </filter>
    <clipPath id="roundClip">
      <rect x="20" y="20" width="533" height="336" rx="16" ry="16"/>
    </clipPath>
  </defs>

  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="573" height="376" rx="16" ry="16" fill="url(#bgGrad)"/>

  <!-- Top accent bar -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="573" height="4" rx="0" fill="url(#accentGrad)"/>

  <!-- Left panel - 3D slice visualization -->
  <g clip-path="url(#roundClip)">

    <!-- Decorative circles (abstract DICOM cross-section) -->
    <circle cx="170" cy="188" r="130" fill="none" stroke="#CBD5E1" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6"/>
    <circle cx="170" cy="188" r="100" fill="none" stroke="#BAE6FD" stroke-width="1.5" opacity="0.7"/>
    <circle cx="170" cy="188" r="70"  fill="none" stroke="#7DD3FC" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.8"/>
    <circle cx="170" cy="188" r="40"  fill="url(#accentGrad)" opacity="0.12"/>

    <!-- Slice lines representing DICOM layers -->
    <g opacity="0.25" stroke="#0369A1" stroke-width="1">
      <line x1="40"  y1="128" x2="300" y2="128"/>
      <line x1="40"  y1="148" x2="300" y2="148"/>
      <line x1="40"  y1="168" x2="300" y2="168"/>
      <line x1="40"  y1="188" x2="300" y2="188"/>
      <line x1="40"  y1="208" x2="300" y2="208"/>
      <line x1="40"  y1="228" x2="300" y2="228"/>
      <line x1="40"  y1="248" x2="300" y2="248"/>
    </g>

    <!-- Active slice highlight -->
    <rect x="40" y="183" width="260" height="10" rx="2" fill="#0EA5E9" opacity="0.15"/>
    <line x1="40" y1="188" x2="300" y2="188" stroke="#0369A1" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.8"/>

    <!-- Scan cross-hairs -->
    <line x1="170" y1="58"  x2="170" y2="318" stroke="#0EA5E9" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4,4" opacity="0.4"/>
    <line x1="40"  y1="188" x2="300" y2="188" stroke="#0EA5E9" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4,4" opacity="0.4"/>

    <!-- STL mesh triangles (right side abstract) -->
    <g fill="none" stroke="#0369A1" stroke-width="1.2" opacity="0.5">
      <polygon points="360,120 400,80  440,120"/>
      <polygon points="400,80  440,120 480,80"/>
      <polygon points="440,120 480,80  520,120"/>
      <polygon points="360,120 400,160 440,120"/>
      <polygon points="400,160 440,120 480,160"/>
      <polygon points="440,120 480,160 520,120"/>
      <polygon points="360,160 400,200 440,160"/>
      <polygon points="400,200 440,160 480,200"/>
      <polygon points="440,160 480,200 520,160"/>
      <polygon points="360,200 400,240 440,200"/>
      <polygon points="400,240 440,200 480,240"/>
      <polygon points="440,200 480,240 520,200"/>
      <polygon points="360,240 400,280 440,240"/>
      <polygon points="400,280 440,240 480,280"/>
      <polygon points="440,240 480,280 520,240"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Mesh faces fill -->
    <g opacity="0.06" fill="#0369A1">
      <polygon points="360,120 400,80  440,120"/>
      <polygon points="440,120 480,80  520,120"/>
      <polygon points="400,160 440,120 480,160"/>
      <polygon points="360,160 400,200 440,160"/>
      <polygon points="440,160 480,200 520,160"/>
      <polygon points="400,240 440,200 480,240"/>
      <polygon points="360,240 400,280 440,240"/>
      <polygon points="440,240 480,280 520,240"/>
    </g>

    <!-- Workflow arrow: DICOM → Segment → STL -->
    <g fill="#64748B" font-size="10" font-family="'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0.8">
      <text x="70"  y="340">DICOM</text>
      <text x="190" y="340">SEGMENT</text>
      <text x="310" y="340">STL</text>
    </g>
    <g stroke="#CBD5E1" stroke-width="1.5" fill="#0EA5E9" opacity="0.7">
      <!-- Arrow 1 -->
      <line x1="100" y1="336" x2="148" y2="336"/>
      <polygon points="148,332 156,336 148,340"/>
      <!-- Arrow 2 -->
      <line x1="225" y1="336" x2="273" y2="336"/>
      <polygon points="273,332 281,336 273,340"/>
    </g>

  </g>

  <!-- App name -->
  <text
     x="286"
     y="72"
     font-family="'Segoe UI', 'SF Pro Display', -apple-system, Arial, sans-serif"
     font-size="42"
     font-weight="700"
     fill="#0F172A"
     text-anchor="middle"
     letter-spacing="-1">MedSlice</text>

  <!-- Tagline -->
  <text
     x="286"
     y="98"
     font-family="'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif"
     font-size="13"
     font-weight="400"
     fill="#64748B"
     text-anchor="middle"
     letter-spacing="0.5">DICOM to STL — Load, Segment, Export</text>

  <!-- Version badge -->
  <rect x="241" y="108" width="90" height="20" rx="10" fill="#E0F2FE"/>
  <text
     x="286"
     y="122"
     font-family="'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif"
     font-size="11"
     font-weight="600"
     fill="#0369A1"
     text-anchor="middle">MVP Build</text>

  <!-- Bottom status bar area -->
  <rect x="0" y="352" width="573" height="24" fill="#FFFFFF" opacity="0.7"/>
  <text
     x="16"
     y="368"
     font-family="'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif"
     font-size="11"
     fill="#94A3B8">Initializing…</text>

  <!-- Accent corner mark -->
  <rect x="540" y="352" width="33" height="24" fill="#0369A1" opacity="0.9"/>
  <text
     x="556"
     y="368"
     font-family="'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif"
     font-size="10"
     font-weight="700"
     fill="#FFFFFF"
     text-anchor="middle">MS</text>
</svg>

content.text:
Pushed. Here's what changed in the design revamp:

**New name: MedSlice**
- `slicer-application-properties.cmake`: `APPLICATION_DISPLAY_NAME` → `"MedSlice"`, description → `"DICOM to STL — Load, Segment, Export"`
- `qSlicerMainWindow.cxx`: Python console title → `"MedSlice Python Console"`
- About dialog picks up the new name automatically via `mainApplicationDisplayName()`

**New design system (Product.qss)**
- Palette: slate-white backgrounds (`#F1F5F9` / `#FFFFFF`), clinical blue accent (`#0369A1`), dark text (`#0F172A`)
- Full coverage: buttons, inputs, combobox, menubar, menus, toolbars, dockwidgets, scrollbars, tabs, groupboxes, trees/lists, sliders, progress bars, statusbar, splitters, checkboxes, tooltips
- Replaces the previous warm-cream terracotta palette

**New splash screen (SVG)**
- Left side: DICOM cross-section visualization with concentric rings and slice lines
- Right side: STL mesh wireframe triangles
- Center top: "MedSlice" wordmark + tagline + "MVP Build" badge
- Bottom: DICOM → SEGMENT → STL workflow indicator with arrows

The Windows build workflow changes remain on `design` only — not merged to `main` since the CI is still being verified.
